info.title: PMXT Hosted Router API
info.description: Hosted-only endpoints for cross-venue search, matching, arbitrage, and SQL.

tags.name: Trading (Hosted)
tags.description: Hosted trading endpoints. Build / submit / cancel orders via `trade.pmxt.dev/v0/*`. Hosted-mode is the default when `pmxt_api_key` is set.

tags.name: Orders & Positions (Hosted)
tags.description: Hosted account reads. Open orders, fills, balances, and positions via `trade.pmxt.dev/v0/*`. Requires `pmxt_api_key` + `wallet_address`.

tags.name: MatchedMarkets
tags.description: Cross-venue matched market and event clusters.

tags.name: SQL
tags.description: Direct read-only SQL access to the catalog (Enterprise).

post.description:
Build a hosted-mode order from catalog `market_id` + `outcome_id`. Returns EIP-712 typed data for the caller to sign locally and then submit via `submitOrderHosted`. The wallet always controls the signature; the hosted server only holds USDC in escrow on behalf of the caller.

See [hosted trading](/concepts/hosted-trading) and the [signing guide](/guides/signing) for end-to-end flow.

200.description: Built order with typed data to sign.

401.description: Invalid or missing PMXT API key.

403.description: Insufficient escrow balance to back the requested order size.

404.description: Outcome not found in the catalog.

422.description: Invalid order parameters (e.g. price out of range, denom mismatch).

x-codeSamples.label: Python
x-codeSamples.source:
import pmxt

client = pmxt.Polymarket(
    pmxt_api_key="YOUR_PMXT_API_KEY",
    wallet_address="0xYourWallet",
    private_key="0x...",
)
built = client.build_order(
    market_id="12345678-1234-1234-1234-123456789abc",
    outcome_id="abcdef01-2345-6789-abcd-ef0123456789",
    side="buy",
    type="limit",
    amount=10,
    price=0.55,
)
print(built.expiry, built.raw)

x-codeSamples.label: TypeScript

post.description:
Submit a signed, previously-built order via `built_order_id`. The order must have been returned from `buildOrderHosted` within the expiry window. Returns the resulting `Order` once execution settles or queues.

For most callers, prefer `createOrderHosted`, which chains build -> sign -> submit in a single call.

200.description: Order accepted by the hosted backend.

401.description: Invalid or missing PMXT API key.

410.description: `built_order_id` expired before submission. Re-run `buildOrderHosted` and submit again.

x-codeSamples.label: Python
x-codeSamples.source:
import pmxt

client = pmxt.Polymarket(
    pmxt_api_key="YOUR_PMXT_API_KEY",
    wallet_address="0xYourWallet",
    private_key="0x...",
)
built = client.build_order(
    market_id="12345678-1234-1234-1234-123456789abc",
    outcome_id="abcdef01-2345-6789-abcd-ef0123456789",
    side="buy",
    type="limit",
    amount=10,
    price=0.55,
)
order = client.submit_order(built)
print(order.id, order.status)

x-codeSamples.label: TypeScript

post.description:
Hosted-mode `createOrder` chains `buildOrderHosted` -> local signing -> `submitOrderHosted` inside the SDK. There is no single HTTP endpoint -- this entry documents the convenience call pattern for SDK users.

If you need to inspect the typed data before signing (e.g. to display order details to an end user before they approve), call `buildOrderHosted` and `submitOrderHosted` directly.

200.description: Order accepted by the hosted backend.

401.description: Invalid or missing PMXT API key.

x-codeSamples.label: Python
x-codeSamples.source:
import pmxt

client = pmxt.Polymarket(
    pmxt_api_key="YOUR_PMXT_API_KEY",
    wallet_address="0xYourWallet",
    private_key="0x...",
)
order = client.create_order(
    market_id="12345678-1234-1234-1234-123456789abc",
    outcome_id="abcdef01-2345-6789-abcd-ef0123456789",
    side="buy",
    type="limit",
    amount=10,
    price=0.55,
)
print(order.id, order.status)

x-codeSamples.label: TypeScript

post.description: Step 1 of the hosted cancel flow. Returns EIP-712 typed data for the caller to sign locally. Step 2 is `POST /v0/orders/cancel` with the resulting signature. The SDK's `cancelOrder()` chains both calls.

401.description: Invalid or missing PMXT API key.

x-codeSamples.label: Python
x-codeSamples.source:
import pmxt

client = pmxt.Polymarket(
    pmxt_api_key="YOUR_PMXT_API_KEY",
    wallet_address="0xYourWallet",
    private_key="0x...",
)
cancelled = client.cancel_order("order_abc123")
print(cancelled.id, cancelled.status)

x-codeSamples.label: TypeScript

get.description: Returns the wallet's escrow USDC balance. Hosted `fetch_balance` returns USDC held inside the PMXT `PreFundedEscrow` contract on behalf of `address`, *not* the venue-native CLOB-proxy balance. See [hosted vs self-hosted](/concepts/hosted-vs-self-hosted) for the distinction.

parameters.name: address
parameters.description: EVM wallet address (lowercase or checksum).

401.description: Invalid or missing PMXT API key.

503.description: Catalog or escrow indexer temporarily unavailable.

x-codeSamples.label: Python
x-codeSamples.source:
import pmxt

client = pmxt.Polymarket(
    pmxt_api_key="YOUR_PMXT_API_KEY",
    wallet_address="0xYourWallet",
)
balance = client.fetch_balance()
print(balance.amount, balance.currency)

x-codeSamples.label: TypeScript

get.description: Returns open positions held by `address` across hosted venues.

parameters.name: address
parameters.description: EVM wallet address.

401.description: Invalid or missing PMXT API key.

x-codeSamples.label: Python
x-codeSamples.source:
import pmxt

client = pmxt.Polymarket(
    pmxt_api_key="YOUR_PMXT_API_KEY",
    wallet_address="0xYourWallet",
)
for position in client.fetch_positions():
    print(position.market_id, position.shares, position.current_value)

x-codeSamples.label: TypeScript

get.description: Returns resting limit orders owned by `address`. Optionally filter by `venue`.

parameters.name: address
parameters.description: EVM wallet address.

parameters.name: venue
parameters.description: Restrict to a single hosted venue.

401.description: Invalid or missing PMXT API key.

x-codeSamples.label: Python
x-codeSamples.source:
import pmxt

client = pmxt.Polymarket(
    pmxt_api_key="YOUR_PMXT_API_KEY",
    wallet_address="0xYourWallet",
)
for order in client.fetch_open_orders():
    print(order.id, order.side, order.price, order.remaining)

x-codeSamples.label: TypeScript

get.description: Returns historical fills for `address`. In hosted mode, closed orders are modelled as trades -- use this endpoint instead of `fetchClosedOrders` / `fetchAllOrders` (which raise `NotSupported`).

parameters.name: address
parameters.description: EVM wallet address.

401.description: Invalid or missing PMXT API key.

x-codeSamples.label: Python
x-codeSamples.source:
import pmxt

client = pmxt.Polymarket(
    pmxt_api_key="YOUR_PMXT_API_KEY",
    wallet_address="0xYourWallet",
)
for trade in client.fetch_my_trades():
    print(trade.id, trade.side, trade.amount, trade.price, trade.tx_hash)

x-codeSamples.label: TypeScript

get.description: Look up a single hosted order by its unified `order_id` (the same string returned by `fetchOpenOrdersHosted`).

parameters.name: order_id
parameters.description: Unified order id.

401.description: Invalid or missing PMXT API key.

x-codeSamples.label: Python
x-codeSamples.source:
import pmxt

client = pmxt.Polymarket(
    pmxt_api_key="YOUR_PMXT_API_KEY",
    wallet_address="0xYourWallet",
)
order = client.fetch_order("order_abc123")
print(order.id, order.status, order.filled, order.remaining)

x-codeSamples.label: TypeScript

get.description: Returns connected clusters of semantically matched events across venues. Use this endpoint to browse or anchor event-level matches while preserving each venue's child markets.

parameters.name: eventId
parameters.description: Anchor the response to a specific event ID.

parameters.name: slug
parameters.description: Anchor the response to a specific event slug.

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: Anchor the response to a specific event URL.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: Text search across cluster titles.

parameters.name: category
parameters.description: Filter both sides of matched edges by event category.

parameters.name: relations
parameters.description: Comma-separated relation filter. Valid values: identity (same resolution), subset (A yes implies B yes), superset (B yes implies A yes), overlap (some shared scenarios), and disjoint (mutually exclusive). Defaults to identity. For subset and superset, direction follows the pairwise edge direction returned in rawMatches when includeRawMatches=true.

parameters.name: relation
parameters.description: Single relation filter. Alias for relations.

parameters.name: venues
parameters.description: Comma-separated venue allow-list.

parameters.name: excludeVenues
parameters.description: Comma-separated venue deny-list.

parameters.name: minVenues
parameters.description: Minimum number of venues required in a cluster.

parameters.name: withOrderbook
parameters.description: Require at least one live orderbook on each matched edge.

parameters.name: updatedSince
parameters.description: Only include matches updated after this timestamp.

parameters.name: includeRawMatches
parameters.description: Include the pairwise match edges used to build each cluster.

parameters.name: edgeLimit
parameters.description: Maximum number of pairwise edges to scan before clustering.

x-codeSamples.label: Python
x-codeSamples.source:
import pmxt

router = pmxt.Router(pmxt_api_key="YOUR_PMXT_API_KEY")
clusters = router.fetch_matched_event_clusters(
    query="Satoshi",
    relation="identity",
    min_venues=2,
    include_raw_matches=True,
    limit=5,
)

for cluster in clusters:
    venues = [event.source_exchange for event in cluster.events]
    print(cluster.canonical_title, venues)

x-codeSamples.label: TypeScript
curl
x-codeSamples.source:
curl -G "https://api.pmxt.dev/v0/matched-event-clusters" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $PMXT_API_KEY" \
  --data-urlencode "query=Satoshi" \
  --data-urlencode "relation=identity" \
  --data-urlencode "minVenues=2" \
  --data-urlencode "includeRawMatches=true" \
  --data-urlencode "limit=5"

get.description: Returns connected clusters of semantically matched markets across venues. Use this endpoint to browse or anchor market-level matches without flattening the response into pairwise rows.

parameters.name: marketId
parameters.description: Anchor the response to a specific market ID.

parameters.name: slug
parameters.description: Anchor the response to a specific market slug.

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: Anchor the response to a specific market URL.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: Text search across cluster titles.

parameters.name: category
parameters.description: Filter both sides of matched edges by market category.

parameters.name: relations
parameters.description: Comma-separated relation filter. Valid values: identity (same resolution), subset (A yes implies B yes), superset (B yes implies A yes), overlap (some shared scenarios), and disjoint (mutually exclusive). Defaults to identity. For subset and superset, direction follows the pairwise edge direction returned in rawMatches when includeRawMatches=true.

parameters.name: relation
parameters.description: Single relation filter. Alias for relations.

parameters.name: venues
parameters.description: Comma-separated venue allow-list.

parameters.name: excludeVenues
parameters.description: Comma-separated venue deny-list.

parameters.name: minVenues
parameters.description: Minimum number of venues required in a cluster.

parameters.name: withOrderbook
parameters.description: Require at least one live orderbook on each matched edge.

parameters.name: updatedSince
parameters.description: Only include matches updated after this timestamp.

parameters.name: includeRawMatches
parameters.description: Include the pairwise match edges used to build each cluster.

parameters.name: edgeLimit
parameters.description: Maximum number of pairwise edges to scan before clustering.

x-codeSamples.label: Python
x-codeSamples.source:
import pmxt

router = pmxt.Router(pmxt_api_key="YOUR_PMXT_API_KEY")
clusters = router.fetch_matched_market_clusters(
    query="Satoshi",
    relation="identity",
    min_venues=2,
    include_raw_matches=True,
    limit=5,
)

for cluster in clusters:
    venues = [market.source_exchange for market in cluster.markets]
    print(cluster.canonical_title, venues)

x-codeSamples.label: TypeScript
curl
x-codeSamples.source:
curl -G "https://api.pmxt.dev/v0/matched-market-clusters" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $PMXT_API_KEY" \
  --data-urlencode "query=Satoshi" \
  --data-urlencode "relation=identity" \
  --data-urlencode "minVenues=2" \
  --data-urlencode "includeRawMatches=true" \
  --data-urlencode "limit=5"

post.summary: Execute a read-only SQL query against ClickHouse

message.example: SQL query access requires an Enterprise plan
Query exceeded the maximum execution time (5s)
SQL query service is not available

bearerAuth.description: Required when calling the hosted API directly (curl, requests, fetch). SDK users pass credentials via constructor params instead.

HostedErrorResponse.description: Error envelope returned by `trade.pmxt.dev/v0/*` endpoints. Mirrors the `ErrorDetail` shape used inside `BaseResponse.error` from the sidecar spec.

BuildOrderHostedRequest.description: Hosted build-order request. Keys mirror the SDK constructor of an `Order`: pass catalog UUIDs (`market_id`, `outcome_id`), not venue-native ids.

amount.description: Order size. For `market` buys, in USDC; for `market` sells / `limit`, in shares.

user_address.description: EVM wallet address that will sign the resulting typed data.

BuildOrderHostedResponse.description: Hosted build-order response. The caller must sign `typed_data` locally (and `pull_typed_data` if present) and POST the signatures back via `submitOrderHosted` before the order expires.

built_order_id.description: Opaque server-side key used by `submitOrderHosted` to look up the build context.

typed_data.description: EIP-712 typed data for the order. Sign locally with the wallet that matches `user_address`.

pull_typed_data.description: Optional secondary EIP-712 payload for venues that require a pull-authorization (e.g. Polymarket neg-risk markets).

quote.description: Pre-trade quote: expected average fill price, slippage, and fees.

resolved.description: Venue-native fields resolved from the catalog UUIDs (`venue`, `token_id`, `neg_risk`, `tick_size`).

SubmitOrderHostedRequest.description: Hosted submit-order request. `signature` is the local EIP-712 signature over `BuildOrderHostedResponse.typed_data`.

pull_signature.description: Signature for the secondary pull-authorization, required when `pull_typed_data` was returned.

wait.description: When `true`, the server waits for on-chain settlement before responding.

order_id.description: Unified order id, as returned by `fetchOpenOrdersHosted`.

deadline.description: Unix epoch (s) after which the cancel build expires.

OrderV0.description: Hosted-mode `Order` shape. Mirrors `pmxt.Order` so the SDK can return it directly. `tx_hash`, `chain`, and `block_number` populate once execution settles on-chain.

tx_hash.description: On-chain transaction hash. Null until settled.

PositionV0.description: Hosted-mode `Position` shape. `current_price`, `current_value`, `entry_price`, `realized_pnl`, and `outcome_label` may be null when the server doesn't yet have the data.

BalanceV0.description: Hosted-mode `Balance` shape. Returns USDC held inside the PMXT `PreFundedEscrow` contract.

venue.description: Set when the hosted backend returns a per-venue breakdown; null when balance is venue-agnostic.
